items.title: Khaled to meet Norwegian counterpart over blocked naval missile delivery
items.summary: KLUANG: Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin will meet his Norwegian counterpart in Singapore later this month to seek clarification on Norway's surprise decision to block the export licence for naval strike missiles (NSM) to Malaysia. He said the meeting would also be used to express the Malaysian government's regret and surprise at the cancellation, which occurred just days before the scheduled delivery in March. "But a few days before delivery, without any indication, the Norwegian government announced it would not grant the marketing licence after eight years.

items.title: Development impact more vital than total spending, says Hajiji
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Ensuring development reaches communities across Sabah is more important than simply exhausting the RM12.02 billion ceiling approved for the state. Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said the allocation, the largest under the 13th Malaysia Plan, was intended to fund 1,173 programmes and projects. "Across Sabah, we are seeing strong momentum in infrastructure development, from roads and healthcare facilities to utilities and digital connectivity.

items.title: KTC unveils RM150mil industrial park blueprint in Sabah
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Kim Teck Cheong Consolidated Bhd (KTC) has unveiled its long-term development blueprint for the RM150 million KTC Industrial Park at the Kota Kinabalu Industrial Park here. https://www.nst.com.my/business/corporate/2025/06/1229227/ktc-buys-rm40mil-plot-sabah-industrial-hub Strategically positioned within KKIP, the industrial park is expected to enhance logistical efficiency while supporting KTC's long-term expansion plans across Sabah, Malaysia and the wider Asean market. The groundbreaking ceremony of the KTC Industrial Park was officiated by Deputy Chief Minister III and Industrial Development, Entrepreneurship and Transport Minister B Datuk Ewon Benedick.

items.title: Malaysia’s unemployment rate holds steady at 2.9pc in March, says DOSM
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 12 — Malaysia’s labour market remained stable in March 2026, with the unemployment rate unchanged at 2.9 per cent, supported by continued expansion in employment and labour force participation, according to the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM). “The labour force increased by 0.1 per cent to 17.31 million persons in March 2026 from 17.30 million in February, while the labour force participation rate (LFPR) remained at 70.9 per cent,” he said in a statement today. Mohd Uzir said the number of employed persons also edged up by 0.1 per cent to 16.80 million persons compared with 16.79 million recorded in the previous month.

items.title: Yeo Bee Yin cautions timing of fuel subsidy cuts amid fragile economy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Removing RON95 fuel subsidies for higher-income groups could trigger a broader economic downturn, DAP National Publicity Secretary Yeo Bee Yin warned. Responding to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's announcement to cut subsidies for the T15 and T20 categories, she said the timing was detrimental to a fragile economy. Yeo said fuel price increases would ripple through the supply chain.

items.title: Woman, 76, duped out of RM327,600 by scammers posing as police officers
items.summary: KUANTAN, May 12 — A 76-year-old woman lost RM327,600 after falling victim to a phone scam syndicate posing as police officers. Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the victim received a call from an individual posing as a police officer from Sarawak, who claimed that her identity card number had been linked to fraudulent activities and that her bank account was under investigation. Yahaya said the victim made 42 transactions totalling RM327,600 into seven different accounts between May 28, 2025, and March 25 this year, using her savings and loans from moneylending companies.

items.title: Bribery attempt fails as Maritime Malaysia detains overloaded sand dredger
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: An attempt to bribe enforcement officers was made after the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) detained a sand dredger for carrying excess cargo off Tanjung Tokong yesterday. Despite the alleged bribery attempt and resistance encountered during the operation, officers proceeded with a full inspection of the vessel. Penang Maritime director Captain Abd Razak Mohamed said the dredger was operated by 17 foreign crew members from Indonesia and China, aged between 32 and 52.

items.title: Senior govt agency executive remanded over RM8,000 bribery probe
items.summary: KUANTAN: A senior construction executive at a government agency has been remanded for allegedly soliciting and accepting RM8,000 in bribes in 2024. The bribes were allegedly received in exchange for helping secure contracts, approving contract evaluations, obtaining work completion certifications, and expediting payment claims for a construction company and a contractor. "Investigations found that the suspect is believed to have committed the offences in 2024 by assisting both companies in securing contracts, approving contract evaluations, obtaining work completion certificates, and expediting payment claims.

items.title: Tangkak murder: "I came to court to see my son's killer."
items.summary: TANGKAK: "I came to court to see who killed my son," said Kim Ang, 60, the mother of Tong Wee Sheng, 38, who was hacked to death at a Bukit Kangkar restaurant two months ago. Kim arrived at the court at 8am today with family after they were informed that three suspects linked to her son's death would be charged. The grieving mother said she was suffering from insomnia and relied on medication to cope since the tragedy.

items.title: Johor Immigration arrests 33 foreigners in raids on 10 premises
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor Immigration Department enforcement officers arrested 33 undocumented foreigners in raids on 10 premises here yesterday. Johor Immigration director Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus said the operation targeted business premises suspected of employing and harbouring migrants illegally. The 33 detainees, aged between 20 and 51, comprised a Nepalese man, four Indonesian men, four Indian men, two Bangladeshi men and 15 Myanmar men.

items.title: Indonesian rupiah falls below 17,500/dollar for first time on US-Iran deadlock
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia's rupiah hit a record low below 17,500 per dollar on Tuesday, leading a broad pullback in emerging Asian currencies, as stalled US-Iran peace negotiations kept oil prices elevated and risk appetite subdued. The Philippine peso dipped 0.8 per cent in its third straight session of losses, while the Indonesian rupiah weakened to an all-time low of 17,510 against the US dollar. The peso and the rupiah have declined nearly 5 per cent and 7 per cent, respectively, since the war in the Middle East began in late February.

items.title: Elderly woman deceived in phone scam loses RM327,600, land titles
items.summary: Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the victim's ordeal began on May 28 last year when she received a phone call from an individual claiming to be a police officer from Sarawak. Yahaya said the suspect assured the victim that the money would be refunded once the investigation was completed. "The victim made 42 transactions totalling RM327,000 into seven bank accounts between May 28 last year and March 25 this year.

items.title: Utility works may have triggered Wangsa Maju sinkhole, says KL mayor; area closed two weeks for repairs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — The sinkhole incident on Jalan Wangsa Delima 4 in Wangsa Maju yesterday is believed to have been caused by utility maintenance works rather than soil subsidence, according to Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL). Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud said investigations were ongoing to determine the exact cause of the incident, while the affected area would remain closed for two weeks for repair works. Initial inspections found that a “sump” wall, or water retention structure, within the area’s main 1.5-metre drainage system had likely collapsed.

items.title: Mechanic charged with stealing clothing, including ladies' underwear, from boutique
items.summary: AYER KEROH: A 21-year-old mechanic was charged at the magistrate's court here today with stealing several items of clothing, including ladies' undergarments, from a boutique last week. The accused, Muhammad Aidil Mustaqim Zulkifeli, pleaded not guilty after the charge was read before magistrate Uthman Abd Ghani. The stolen goods, valued at RM491, included men's T-shirts, women's clothing, brassieres, shorts, and dresses.

items.title: Malaysia's unemployment rate holds steady at 2.9pct in March 2026
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia's labour market remained stable in March 2026, with the unemployment rate unchanged at 2.9 per cent, supported by continued expansion in employment and labour force participation, according to the Statistics Department. "The labour force increased by 0.1 per cent to 17.31 million persons in March 2026 from 17.30 million in February, while the labour force participation rate (LFPR) remained at 70.9 per cent," he said in a statement today. Mohd Uzir said the number of employed persons also edged up by 0.1 per cent to 16.80 million persons compared with 16.79 million recorded in the previous month.

items.title: Budi95 helps Malaysia manage fuel demand amid global uncertainty, says deputy finance minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — The Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) initiative is seen as a useful demand management tool that helps Malaysia understand national usage patterns, said Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong. Liew said this during a fireside chat titled “Malaysia’s Response to Global Energy Crisis: Build Back Better”, moderated by The Edge ’s assistant editor Emir Zainul Ensuring continuous access to fuel supply, even at reduced levels, is important, as political and social stability will enable Malaysia to manage the crisis more confidently, he said. He emphasised that Malaysia is entering the crisis from a position of relative strength, noting that while many countries have seen downward revisions in growth projections after February, Malaysia’s outlook remains resilient.

items.title: Gold could surge to US$5,600 by end-2026 amid global uncertainty, says UBS
items.summary: She said the bullish trajectory remains intact even as gold undergoes a period of consolidation following strong gains in recent years. "We maintain our positive view on gold and still see prices recovering from current levels to print new highs this year," she said during a virtual media briefing on UBS's gold and precious metals outlook. Teves said the key driver remains strong demand from both institutional and official sectors, as gold becomes an increasingly core portfolio asset amid persistent macroeconomic uncertainty.

items.title: Russia, Ukraine resume strikes as truce expires, one dead
items.summary: KYIV: Russia resumed strikes on Ukraine as a three-day truce expired on Tuesday, attacking the capital with drones and killing one person in the eastern region of Dnipropetrovsk, Ukrainian authorities said. The Russian military meanwhile said it had shot down 27 Ukrainian drones after the ceasefire expired. Russian strikes in eastern Ukraine killed one person and wounded at least four others, regional military authorities said.

items.title: ‘What is so difficult?’: Socso CEO questions seven gig platforms yet to submit details for automatic contributions
items.summary: AMPANG, May 12 — Seven of the 14 gig service platform providers have yet to submit their workers’ details to the Social Security Organisation (Socso), delaying the rollout of automatic contributions under the Gig Workers Act 2025. Those named as having completed the process were AirAsia Ride Sdn Bhd, Kiddocare, Eternal Meteor Sdn Bhd (GoGet), ShopeeFood Malaysia, Troopers Innovation Sdn Bhd, Fastgig Sdn Bhd and Delivery Hero Malaysia (Foodpanda) Sdn Bhd. The Gig Workers Act 2025 came into force on March 31, requiring platform providers to register gig workers with Socso and implement automatic 1.25 per cent contributions through platform transactions.

items.title: KL court frees independent preacher Fathi Naim on rape charge involving teenager
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Independent preacher Muhammad Fathi Na’im Mazlam was acquitted and discharged by the Sessions Court here today on a charge of raping a 14-year-old girl five years ago. Judge Tasnim Abu Bakar ruled that the prosecution failed to establish a prima facie case against the 34-year-old, better known as Fathi Naim, at the close of the prosecution’s case. The judge said the testimony of the victim, the third prosecution witness (SP3), could not be regarded as credible after impeachment proceedings revealed material discrepancies between her testimony in this trial and a separate case at the Klang Sessions Court.

items.title: KL flood ponds lost 70pct capacity after land transferred to developers - Yeoh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The capacity of flood retention ponds in Jinjang and Batu have shrunk to about 30 per cent of their original size after 17 land lots were transferred to developers since 2015. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said about 70 per cent of the land area within the water body zones had been transferred for development purposes, reducing the ponds' original size of 33.5 hectares. "The government cannot continue to allow development that compromises the function of flood retention ponds.

items.title: Xi-Trump talks spotlight contrasting styles, expectations
items.summary: DONALD Trump has said he expects a "big, fat hug" from China's Xi Jinping when they meet in Beijing this week, though multiple thorny issues between the two sides could see the US president kept at arm's length. Trump's often bombastic, freewheeling comportment on the international stage contrasts dramatically with Xi's measured, tight-lipped approach to foreign policy. Trump's trip to China, set to begin Wednesday, is the first by a US president since his own in 2017.

items.title: Kuantan International Airport in Gebeng expected to be completed in 2031, says state exco
items.summary: KUANTAN, May 12 — The construction of Kuantan International Airport in Gebeng, here is expected to be completed in 2031, said Pahang Investment, Industry, Science, Technology and Innovation Committee chairman Datuk Mohamad Nizar Najib. He said the project is currently undergoing finalisation on technical requirements, implementation structure and involvement of strategic investors. Mohamad Nizar said the proposed infrastructure development would be implemented through private financing and would not involve state and federal government funds and would be built on land owned by the Pahang Government Secretariat.

items.title: Ekuinas makes RM7.1b in Bumiputera equity value
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Ekuiti Nasional Bhd (Ekuinas) has generated Bumiputera equity value of RM7.1 billion and total shareholder value of RM8.5 billion since its inception, supported by an investment allocation of RM5.1 billion across 49 portfolio companies. This performance was backed by cumulative gains of RM5.7 billion, alongside a strengthened strategic portfolio — including the healthcare sector, which increased to 29 per cent of Ekuinas' active portfolio in 2025. Its proportion of Bumiputera employees in management positions within Ekuinas' portfolio companies rose to 36.9 per cent.

items.title: Malaysia weighs cutting RON95 subsidy cap to 150 litres
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is weighing a further tightening of its subsidised RON95 fuel allocation under the BUDI95 programme, with the monthly cap potentially reduced to 150 litres as part of broader efforts to better manage fuel supply, improve subsidy efficiency and reduce leakage. Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said the proposed adjustment is based on consumption data showing that a large majority of Malaysians use significantly less than the current subsidised threshold. "The data consistently show that 80 per cent of Malaysians use less than 200 litres.

items.title: BUDI95 a key tool in managing fuel demand: Liew
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Budi Madani RON95 (BUDI95) initiative is seen as a useful demand management tool that helps Malaysia understand national usage patterns, said Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong. Liew said this during a fireside chat titled 'Malaysia's Response to Global Energy Crisis: Build Back Better', moderated by The Edge's assistant editor Emir Zainul Ensuring continuous access to fuel supply, even at reduced levels, is important, as political and social stability will enable Malaysia to manage the crisis more confidently, he said. He emphasised that Malaysia is entering the crisis from a position of relative strength, noting that while many countries have seen downward revisions in growth projections after February, Malaysia's outlook remains resilient.

items.title: Beyond trade: Zahid says Malaysia, China to co-build future industries through TVET
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Malaysia and China will deepen cooperation to co-develop future industries, with Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) serving as Malaysia’s first line of readiness for the future economy, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. He said both countries must move beyond traditional trade, identifying electric mobility, battery technology, renewable energy, smart manufacturing, digital systems and talent development as key areas of collaboration. China brings technological depth, industrial scale and proven leadership in new energy industries.

items.title: Malaysia seeks answers after Norway halts naval missile delivery despite eight-year deal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Malaysia has been left “shocked” after Norway halted the delivery of missiles ordered in 2018 for the Royal Malaysian Navy’s Littoral Combat Ships (LCS), Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said today. According to The Star, Khaled said the missiles, which were scheduled for delivery in March this year, were blocked after the Norwegian government refused to approve the export licence just days before shipment. “Export licensing decisions are handled by the Norwegian authorities.

items.title: Wet weather alert: MetMalaysia warns of thunderstorms, heavy rain in nine states until 3pm today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected in several areas across nine states until 3pm today, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). In a weather warning issued at noon, MetMalaysia said the affected areas in Kedah are Langkawi, Sik and Baling, while in Penang, the warning covers the Barat Daya and Timur Laut districts. In Perak, the affected areas are Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak and Batang Padang.

items.title: Two safe after light aircraft accident involving emergency landing in Langkawi, says CAAM
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 12 — The Civil Aviation Authority of Malaysia (CAAM) confirms an accident had occurred involving a Diamond Aircraft model DA-40 bearing registration number 9M-HMJ on a training flight, in Langkawi today. It said the aircraft conducted an emergency landing in the vicinity of Kuah. “The aircraft departed Langkawi International Airport at 9.54am for a scheduled training flight, and the last communication with Air Traffic Control Tower Langkawi was recorded at 10.29am,” it said in a statement.

items.title: Petronas LNG, MISC ink 20-year charter deal for five new LNG carriers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Petronas LNG Ltd has signed a 20-year time charter agreement with MISC Group for five newbuild 174,000-cbm liquefied natural gas carriers, strengthening its long-term supply reliability while supporting the energy transition towards a lower-carbon future. The agreement was signed by Petronas LNG chief executive officer Ezran Mahadzir and MISC vice president of Gas Asset and Solutions Hazrin Hasan. Petronas executive vice president and chief executive officer of gas and maritime business Datuk Adif Zulkifli said the addition of these new LNG carriers marks another important milestone as the group continues leveraging the collective strengths of its businesses to create long-term value across the LNG value chain.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues thunderstorm warning for nine states until 3pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds are expected to persist across several areas in nine states until 3pm today. In a weather warning issued at noon, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said the affected areas in Kedah include Langkawi, Sik, and Baling, while the Barat Daya and Timur Laut districts in Penang are also on alert. In Perak, the warning covers Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak, and Batang Padang.

items.title: Pollution in Sungai Kereh caused by multiple factors, not just pig farms, says Penang exco
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 12 — The longstanding pollution issue in Sungai Kereh in Kampung Selamat, Seberang Perai Utara, is caused by multiple factors such as agricultural activities, drainage systems and waste discharge from various sources. State local government and town and country planning committee chairman H’ng Mooi Lye said the pollution was not only caused by pig farming activities. H’ng said the Penang Department of Environment (DoE) had received seven complaints related to water pollution and foul odour in Sungai Kereh over the past two years, from 2024 to 2026.

items.title: Malaysia–China to invest RM500mil in high TVET push for EV and advanced tech talent
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia and China will allocate more than RM500 million to develop students under High Technical and Vocational Education and Training (High TVET) through the Malaysia–China Institute (MCI). Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the collaboration between Malaysia and China includes the provision of various advanced technology equipment for learning and training purposes. He said Malaysia is shifting its TVET ecosystem beyond conventional education towards high TVET, which focuses on advanced technological skills and industry-driven training.

items.title: Seven gig platforms yet to submit worker data to Perkeso
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Seven major gig platform providers have yet to submit workers' data to the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) despite the enforcement of the Gig Workers Act 2025 (Act 872). Mohammed Azman said the seven platform providers that had submitted workers' data to Perkeso were Airasia Ride Sdn. Bhd.

items.title: Four decades on, Sungai Kreh pollution still unresolved
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Pollution in Sungai Kreh remains a persistent environmental concern despite multiple enforcement actions, with seven complaints involving water contamination and foul odour recorded between 2024 and 2026. State Local Government Committee chairman H'ng Mooi Lye said the river continues to fall under the polluted to moderately polluted category based on monitoring conducted under the Environmental Quality Monitoring Programme. Water samples were collected and analysed by the Chemistry Department," he said.

items.title: Malaysia fast-tracks semiconductor listings on Bursa to align market with industry growth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Finance Ministry is beefing up efforts to reshape Malaysia's capital market by establishing a clearer and more structured pathway for semiconductor-related companies to list on Bursa Malaysia, in a strategic push to align the equity market more closely with the country's fast-growing industrial and technology sectors. Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said discussions were already underway with the Securities Commission managing director Datin Paduka Azalina Adham and Bursa Malaysia Bhd chief executive officer Datuk Fad'l Mohamed. Liew said the focus includes multinational semiconductor firms operating in Malaysia and locally grown companies within the broader semiconductor ecosystem.

items.title: CME urges major subsidy overhaul, proposes shift to voucher-based support model
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia should overhaul its rigid, tiered subsidy framework and replace it with a more flexible, market-based voucher system to improve targeting and reduce economic distortions, the Centre for Market Education (CME) said. The think tank argued that current subsidy mechanisms risk misallocating support by relying heavily on income classifications while also distorting market prices at the point of sale. Need is not captured by income alone," Dr Carmelo Ferlito, chief executive officer of CME, said.

items.title: Americans don't think Trump has explained Iran war goals, poll shows
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Two out of three Americans think President Donald Trump has not clearly explained why the country went to war with Iran, according to a Reuters/Ipsos poll completed on Monday that also showed his approval rating ticking up from the lowest level of his term. More than two months into a conflict that began Feb 28 with a US-Israeli bombing campaign, some 66 per cent of poll respondents - including one in three Republicans and almost all Democrats - said Trump has not "clearly explained the goals of US military involvement in Iran." Some 36 per cent of Americans approve of Trump's performance, up two percentage points since a late April Reuters/Ipsos poll showed his approval rating at 34 per cent, which was the lowest level of Trump's current term in office.

items.title: [UPDATED] Preacher acquitted of raping 14-year-old girl five years ago
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Sessions Court today acquitted and discharged independent preacher Muhammad Fathi Na'im Mazlam of raping a 14-year-old girl at a hotel five years ago. Tasnim, in her ruling, said the court found the testimony of the victim, who was the third prosecution witness, was not credible. She said the court had allowed a defence application to challenge the witness's credibility during cross-examination.

items.title: [UPDATED] Light aircraft makes emergency landing in Langkawi, two safe
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Two people escaped unharmed after a light aircraft made an emergency landing near Kuah, Langkawi, today. The Civil Aviation Authority of Malaysia (CAAM) said the incident involved a Diamond Aircraft DA-40, registration 9M-HMJ, which was on a training flight. Langkawi district police chief Assistant Commissioner Khairul Azhar Nuruddin said the aircraft was carrying a male instructor and a female trainee.

items.title: Don't turn middle class into govt's 'ATM', Lim warns over RON95 subsidy reform
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kepong MP Lim Lip Eng has urged the government to clearly explain the proposed RON95 fuel subsidy reform, warning that the middle class must not become the government's "ATM". He said if subsidies were to be removed or reduced for higher-income groups, Putrajaya must first disclose who would be affected, how income would be assessed, and whether factors such as living costs, dependants, loans and daily travel needs would be considered. he said.

items.title: Johor PH-BN cooperation direction to be clarified at weekend convention, says Amanah leader
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 12 — The upcoming Pakatan Harapan (PH) convention here this weekend will be an important platform to determine the direction of cooperation between the coalition and Barisan Nasional (BN) ahead of the election, said Johor Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) vice-chief Suhaizan Kaiat. The Pulai MP said the convention needs to clarify the current political position and strategy involving the unity government in the state. “This convention is very important in providing a clear direction for PH to face the upcoming state election and general election,” he said in a statement issued today.

items.title: Sarawak free tertiary education scheme benefits 4,694 students, intake expected to rise
items.summary: KUCHING, May 12 — A total of 4,694 students from four higher education institutions (IPTs) in Sarawak have been offered free education through the Sarawak Free Higher Education Scheme (FTES) so far. Sarawak Deputy Minister of Education, Innovation and Talent Development Datuk Dr Annuar Rapaee said that according to the breakdown, i-CATS University College involved 172 students, Curtin University Malaysia (543 students), Swinburne University of Technology Sarawak Campus (1,959 students) and Universiti Teknologi Sarawak (2,020 students). Meanwhile, Dr Annuar said a total of 1,433 students chose not to accept the free education offer.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh: KL’s flood retention pond now 70pc smaller as land lost to developers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Seventeen land lots within the Jinjang and Batu flood retention ponds have been alienated to developers since 2015, causing an estimated 70 per cent shrinkage of the ponds’ capacity. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh, when disclosing the problem, urged the government to take the issue seriously and ordered the stoppage of any development applications on the land there. “The Madani government cannot continue to allow development that compromises the function of flood retention ponds,” Yeoh said at a press conference here.

items.title: Pahang approves 18 flood mitigation projects worth RM3.18b, says MB
items.summary: KUANTAN, May 12 — The Pahang government has approved and is implementing 18 flood mitigation projects across the state involving a total cost of RM3.184 billion. According to Sinar Harian , Mentri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail said the large-scale projects are being carried out under the 12th Malaysia Plan (RMK-12) and 13th Malaysia Plan (RMK-13). “Among the projects currently being actively implemented are RTB Bandar Pekan, the Integrated River Basin Development (PLSB) Sungai Kuantan, as well as RTB Sungai Semantan in Temerloh.

items.title: MMEA rescues 23 undocumented migrants after boat capsizes off Pulau Pangkor, search continues for others
items.summary: Perak MMEA director Maritime Captain Mohamad Shukri Khotob said the agency received a report at about 5.30am from a local fisherman who discovered several victims floating at sea and immediately sought assistance. Mohamad Shukri said preliminary investigations revealed that 37 PATI were believed to have been on board the boat. In a separate development, Mohamad Shukri said the MMEA detained a Class C fishing vessel during an enforcement operation conducted last Sunday.

items.title: Police still probing alleged Langkawi drug network linked to cop known as ‘Mr A’, says IGP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail today said authorities are still identifying individuals allegedly involved in a drug distribution network operating in Langkawi, including a police officer known only as “Mr A”. Speaking to reporters after launching the 'PDRM Special Dialogue V 2026', Mohd Khalid said the matter remains under investigation and police are unable to comment further for now. Asked whether police personnel found to be involved would be investigated, Mohd Khalid said authorities would not hesitate to take action.

items.title: On International Nurses Day, Anwar pays tribute to nursing community for invaluable service, sacrifice
items.summary: In a Facebook post in conjunction with International Nurses Day today, the prime minister said the contributions of nurses often go beyond words, and their sacrifices are invaluable to the people and the nation. Happy International Nurses Day,” he said. International Nurses Day is celebrated on May 12 each year to recognise the contributions of nurses to communities around the world.

items.title: Teenager charged with murder of college student found with 61 stab wounds in Kelantan
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 12 — A teenager was charged in the Magistrates’ Court here today with the murder of a female college student whose body was found with 61 stab wounds about two weeks ago. Muhammad Hazim Muhammad, 19, nodded after the charge was read out before Magistrate Rais Imran Hamid. The prosecution was conducted by Kelantan State Prosecution director Wan Azimah Yaacob, together with deputy public prosecutors Muhamad Nadzir Abdullah and Ahmad Naabil Mohamed Asri, while the accused was represented by lawyer Mohd Alias Ibrahim.

items.title: Penang ramps up heatwave, storm preparedness ahead of El Niño, focuses on vulnerable groups
items.summary: State Housing and Environment Committee chairman Datuk Seri S. Sundarajoo (PH-Perai) said the state government, through the Disaster Management Unit (UPB) and Think City, has developed community preparedness guidelines for heatwaves and floods under the Penang Nature-Based Climate Adaptation Programme (PNBCAP). He said the initiative aims to improve awareness, preparedness and community resilience among groups with limited access to information on climate-related risks. Sundarajoo said the state had also organised several disaster preparedness programmes this year, including the Penang Disaster Operations Management Workshop 2026 Series 1 focusing on maritime disaster response involving passenger ship fires at sea.

items.title: Penang shifting to high-value investments, digital economy to cut reliance on federal funds, says CM
items.summary: He said the strategy would enable the state to generate direct revenue through land premiums, rentals and related economic activities. He said the state government is also accelerating the digitalisation of public services, including revenue collection systems, to improve efficiency and reduce leakages. He said the state is also reviewing fees and charges periodically, enforcing the collection of outstanding payments and monetising state assets as part of efforts to strengthen non-tax revenue sources.

items.title: Penang open to hosting music rave festivals with right venues, budget, sponsors, says exco
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 12 — The Penang state government is ready to host a music rave festival in the state with appropriate locations, budget and sponsors, said state exco Wong Hon Wai. The state tourism and creative economy committee chairman said the state is open to hosting a variety of music festivals provided it is in a suitable location, with an appropriate budget and sponsors. Wong said Penang is focused on promoting experiential tourism to visitors on top of promoting the existing tourism attractions in the state.

items.title: Anwar congratulates ‘friend’ Vijay after Indian actor takes helm as Tamil Nadu chief minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today congratulated Tamil actor C. Joseph Vijay for becoming the new chief minister of Tamil Nadu following his stunning political debut recently. Hailing Vijay as his “friend”, Anwar said the 51-year-old actor’s Oru Viral Puratchi — or “one-finger revolution” in Tamil — is currently “on the cusp of creating history”. The Oru Viral Puratchi refers to Vijay’s 2018 political blockbuster, Sarkar, which depicted Vijay as a prominent business magnate entering politics to take down a corrupt government.

items.title: Yeo Bee Yin warns RON95 subsidy cut for T20 could fuel inflation and hurt economy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Puchong MP Yeo Bee Yin has cautioned that it is the “wrong time” to remove RON95 fuel subsidies for higher-income Malaysians, warning of broader inflationary and economic risks. Yeo said the T15 and T20 groups are also key drivers of domestic private consumption, and higher fuel costs could dampen demand in retail, hospitality and services sectors. Yeo said the government must consider the broader fiscal impact, including potential stimulus spending required to offset economic slowdown.

items.title: Police bust Manjung online investment scam syndicate, arrest 13 Chinese nationals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Police have busted an online investment scam syndicate operating through a call centre following three separate raids in Sitiawan and Lumut on May 8, according to Sinar Harian . Manjung police chief ACP Hasbullah Abd Rahman said the raids were carried out by officers and personnel from the district police headquarters’ Commercial Crime Investigation Division and Narcotics Criminal Investigation Division. Hasbullah said initial investigations found the syndicate was using the premises as a “calling centre” to run online investment scams.

items.title: ‘How much longer?’ Bukit Antarabangsa rep presses for delayed EKVE Phase 2 to be finished
items.summary: He said delays to the project are causing residents to continue facing traffic congestion, unsafe road conditions and safety risks at construction sites, including mudflow and the potential for landslides. The visit was also attended by the Malaysian Highway Authority director-general Mohd Hadzmir Yusoff, the Prime Minister’s Department Public-Private Partnership Unit director-general Muhammad Razman Abu Samah and WZR Group executive chairman Wan Nadzir Wan Mohamad. Mohd Kamri said a site visit yesterday showed that much of the construction work was still incomplete, with work reportedly halted for the past week.

items.title: Four Bukit Indah homes hit in suspected Molotov cocktail attacks linked to Singapore Ah Long syndicate
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, May 12 — Police have confirmed receiving reports of four houses in Bukit Indah here that were set ablaze, believed to have been targeted in petrol bomb (Molotov cocktail) attacks on Friday and Saturday. In a statement, Iskandar Puteri district police headquarters said investigations are being carried out under Section 5(2) of the Moneylenders Act 1951 and Section 435 of the Penal Code. In a Facebook post, Liew expressed his appreciation to Bukit Indah police station chief Inspector J.M.

items.title: Mechanic changing punctured tyre killed in Temerloh LPT lorry crash
items.summary: KUANTAN, May 12 — A mechanic was killed after he was struck by a lorry while checking a punctured tyre on a trailer at KM119.5 of the East Coast Expressway (LPT) heading west in Temerloh yesterday. He said initial investigations showed the trailer had stopped in the emergency lane due to a punctured rear tyre. The 36-year-old lorry driver and the 35-year-old trailer driver escaped unhurt.

items.title: ‘Don’t turn middle class into govt’s ATM’, Kepong MP tells Putrajaya over RON95 subsidy reform
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 12 — Kepong MP Lim Lip Eng today urged the government to fully explain its proposed RON95 fuel subsidy reform, warning that middle-class Malaysians should not be penalised under a poorly defined mechanism. In a statement today, Lim said Putrajaya must clearly disclose who would be affected by the subsidy rationalisation plan, how income would be assessed, and whether factors such as living costs, dependants, loans and commuting needs would be taken into account. Lim said the government must avoid relying solely on income figures while ignoring actual household burdens faced by families.
